orgPolicy.title: California Relocation Documents — New Dropdown Required
orgPolicy.summary: Effective June 18, a new dropdown now appears in Greenhouse when candidates opt into relocation. Recruiters must select whether the destination is California — this determines which relocation/clawback documents are generated (California Bill 692 compliance).
bullets: New dropdown appears in GH when candidate opts into relocation
Select 'Yes' if relocating to California; 'No' if not
California requires specific legal (clawback) language per Bill 692
Effective June 18, 2026

tp.point: New field in GH for CA relocation — make sure you’re selecting correctly or the wrong docs get generated.

orgPolicy.title: Broker License Verbiage Updated in Offer Letters
orgPolicy.summary: For CX and Sales roles requiring a broker license, updated verbiage now populates in offer letters when 'Yes' is selected on the broker license question. Verbiage covers 3-month completion window, reassignment risk, and reimbursement terms.
bullets: Applies to CX and Sales roles with a broker license requirement
New verbiage: 3-month timeline, possible reassignment if not completed, and reimbursement policy
California law carveouts included where applicable

tp.audience: Recruiters on CX and Sales roles
tp.point: Broker license verbiage in offer letters has been updated — check the IOPE doc for the new language.

orgPolicy.title: Comp Bands GSheets Gone — Use Workday Report
orgPolicy.summary: The legacy comp bands Google Sheets are no longer functioning. PeopleTech built a replacement Workday report. All job reqs now have comp bands assigned in Workday, feeding downstream into Greenhouse and PeopleOS. New Hire grant data still in development.
bullets: Bookmark: https://wd5.myworkday.com/gusto/d/inst/1422$5089/rel-task/3001$14.htmld
Search by Job Family; returns all ranges across levels and comp zones
All reqs now have bands; GH and PeopleOS reflect this
New Hire grant info still in development

tp.point: Comp bands GSheets are gone — everyone needs the Workday report now. Bookmark the link.

orgPolicy.title: Offers Over Max of Mature — Comp Required, Loop In Brian
orgPolicy.summary: Reminder: any offer going over the max of mature must include comp justification. PEs must contact Brian before proceeding. A hiring PE recently wasn’t aware, so cascading as a reminder.
bullets: Any offer over max of mature requires comp review
PE must reach out to Brian before proceeding
Applies to all pods

tp.point: Reminder: offers over max of mature need comp involved — loop Brian in before moving forward.

orgPolicy.title: Unhire Process Change — Recruiters No Longer Execute Directly
orgPolicy.summary: Recruiters will no longer need to process unhires themselves. Michelle Eakin’s team takes it from here. The process remains the same: ping in #invite_notifications.
bullets: Ping #invite_notifications as before
Michelle Eakin’s team picks up and executes the unhire
No change to recruiter workflow entry point

tp.point: Good news: you no longer have to process unhires directly. Ping #invite_notifications and it’s handled.

orgPolicy.title: Process/System Change Flag Protocol — Submit Tickets Early
orgPolicy.summary: IOPE flagged an uptick in last-minute system change requests. Any process changes that require GH configuration (custom fields, verbiage, etc.) must be submitted via go/phc ticket early. Rush changes carry quality risk.
bullets: Covers: custom offer detail fields, verbiage changes, any GH configuration
Submit a go/phc ticket when in doubt
Rush changes put quality and testing at risk — flag early

items.heading: C2 Is Live — Kicked Off June 15
bullets: Six Invite C2 initiatives: Map TA Skill Gaps; Define L&D Roadmap & Learning Facilitation Plan; Run End-to-End Recruiter AI Workflow Discovery; Design & Pilot Standardized Intake Process; Build V1 Centralized Recruiting Reporting Dashboard; Headcount & Capacity Planning Discovery & Framework
Kristin and Laura are modeling in-office/hub-based hiring numbers this week; results coming to People Staff
InviteOS and PeopleOS features continue to roll out alongside C2 work

items.heading: FY27 Company OKRs — People Team Owns Objective 5
bullets: Objective 5: 'Grow Impact per Gustie' — 4 KRs currently being scoped
Several OKRs directly affect how we hire; more detail to come

items.heading: New Head of Talent Development: Erin Waldron
bullets: Joins from Amazon (~6 years) with a product management lens on talent work
C2 priorities: redesigning compliance training and mapping the PE user journey
Close partner to Invite going forward

items.heading: Finance FinClose AI Demo — Relevant to Invite’s AI Path
bullets: Finance built an agentic platform to automate their monthly close; days of work now takes minutes
Design principle: AI off individual desktops and into a governed, durable platform with human review at key gates
Scott shared this with People Team specifically because Invite is on the same path in C2

items.heading: Welcome Party Confirmed: Bierstadt Lagerhaus
bullets: Private bar (3 hrs) + buffet (2 hrs) + large games; timing 5–8 or 5:30–8:30 PM
4 teams of 10 (one team of 11); Jaime owning bookings
Hackathon pre-work and Tuesday activity agenda still TBD — PEs asked to weigh in

items.heading: Recruiters Asked to Log In Daily and Submit Feedback
bullets: PeopleOS is live — log in via Okta every day
New features actively rolling in; team’s feedback directly shapes what gets built
Use the 'Share Feedback' button inside the app
Direct link: https://people-os.gusto.com/

items.heading: Laura Lockwood Starting Leave June 23 — Elan Bailey Covering
bullets: Elan being added to PE meetings and #invite-pes during leave
Kristin remains primary point of contact

items.heading: AI Usage on Invite — Shared Language Coming Before QPRs
bullets: Kristin flagged the need for a shared definition: what does ‘integrator’ mean on Invite re: AI?
Goal: united baseline POV across pods before QPR conversations
PEs will cover this with their teams in advance of QPRs

items.heading: VMA Practice Simulator Pilot — 1 Nominee Needed Per Team by 7/22
bullets: Lisa requesting 1 recruiter from each PE team to pilot the VMA Practice Simulator
Simulator lets interviewers practice signal ID, rubric application, and scorecard writing
Nominees due to Lisa by Monday July 22; she’ll follow up with access and next steps

items.heading: Design Thinking Workshop Outputs Informing C2 Workstreams
bullets: Last week’s Invite Workflow Design Thinking Workshop themes are directly shaping C2 priorities
Deck updated to include Group 1 content on hiring committee
PE team reviewing inputs over coming weeks; more sessions planned for future
